original_abstract: In this article, we focus on manifest interparty conflict over policy issues and the role of coalition agreements in solving these conflicts. We present empirical findings on the characteristics of coalition agreements including deals over policy controversy and on inter-party conflict occurring during the lifetime of governments in Germany, Belgium, Italy and the Netherlands. We analyze the ways in which parties in government were or were not constrained by written deals over disputed issues. Coalition agreements from all four countries include specific policy deals, one third of which are precisely defined. These policy deals concern both consensual and controversial issues. Our central finding is that, in the case of intra-party conflict, parties almost always fall back on the initial policy deals when these exist. As such, policy statements of the coalition agreement facilitate decision making in each of the countries studied.
humanized_abstract: This paper focuses on manifestations of interparty conflict arising from policy differences and on the role of coalition agreements in resolving such conflicts. Using data on characteristics of coalition agreements (policy conflict included) and interparty conflict occurring in the course of the life cycle of successive West German, Belgian, Italian and Dutch coalition governments, the paper analyses whether and how governments' activities were influenced by the written policy deals agreed upon in advance by the participating parties. All of the agreements we examined contained a certain number of policy deals related to controversy over specific policies, some of a constitutional nature and others less so. About a third of the policy deals were directly related to highly salient policy controversies and involved either consensual or highly contested policy issues. In each of the countries that were included in our analysis, the policy statements contained in the coalition agreement were important in helping parties arrive at decisions concerning their collective governance activities. In most cases, when intra-party conflicts arose, these issues were resolved by a return to the terms of the initial policy deal agreed upon in the coalition negotiations of the party in question - whenever such terms existed.
